methods.description: A correlation analysis was performed to assess the strength of the relationship between the pollen collected by the Pollensniffer and the static pollen sampler.

steps.description: A Shapiro-Wilk test showed that neither the original nor the log-transformed pollen data of both samplers were normally distributed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Shapiro-Wilk test for normality on pollen data

methods.description: A regression analysis was performed to calculate the average difference between the pollen collected by the Pollensniffer and the static sampler.

steps.description: Regression analysis was performed using the counts of all individual pollen types.
steps.searchable_summary: perform regression analysis on pollen counts

methods.description: A one way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was performed on the log transformed data to compare the 5 individual Pollensniffers.

steps.description: Mean and standard deviations of the 8 measurements were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean and standard deviation of measurements

steps.description: A one way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was performed on the log transformed data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one way ANOVA on log transformed data

methods.description: Comparison between rooftop level and street level measurements, and timing of the first seasonal pollen collected.

steps.description: Daily concentrations of the static sampler were compared to the sum of the street level measurements of the three locations in the morning, the early afternoon and the early evening.
steps.searchable_summary: compare daily concentrations from static sampler to street level measurements

steps.description: Compared the day at which more than 3 grains/m3 were collected by the static sampler and when more than 3 grains were collected by the Pollensniffer.
steps.searchable_summary: compare first day of more than 3 grains/m3 collection between samplers
